title: Failure Classification, Retry Budgets, Backoff, and Load Shedding · Contract Lens: Backoff Jitter Scheduling
summary: Every exception is requeued immediately, permanent schema failures block healthy work, a database outage causes a retry storm, and attempt counts exist only in process memory. Planned artifact: Add and verify the backoff jitter scheduling increment in failure taxonomy and bounded retry policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply exponential backoff and jitter without blocking consumer slots or requeueing hot loops and preserve intended availability time.
Bound attempts, total age, delay, processing deadline, queue residence, and operator escalation as one retry budget.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Predict Backoff Jitter Scheduling · Theory Backoff Jitter Scheduling
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory messaging team handles case 5068 in a multi-tenant case event backbone with three subscriber responsibilities. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify permanent and ambiguous outcomes before applying both attempt and age budgets; bound payload, buffer, and retained-ledger bytes; then reuse one message identity with a different payload fingerprint. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
